senses.definition: Changed into a more English form in language, style, or character.
examples: The town’s original name was later anglified to make it easier for English speakers to pronounce.
Many foreign words have been anglified over time in everyday English.
Her surname was anglified when her grandparents moved to the UK.

senses.definition: Changes something into a more English form in language, style, or character.
examples: The translator often anglifies place names in the novel.
This spelling anglifies the original French word.
The director anglifies the story to appeal to a British audience.

senses.definition: To make something English in language, style, or character.
examples: Editors sometimes anglify foreign names for English readers.
They chose not to anglify the original title of the film.
The company tried to anglify its brand to fit the UK market.

senses.definition: Making something more English in language, style, or character.
examples: By anglifying the characters’ names, the author hoped to reach a wider audience.
The process of anglifying foreign words can change their pronunciation.
They are anglifying their marketing materials for the British market.

senses.definition: A person who has an extreme or obsessive admiration for England or English things.
examples: He was an anglimaniac who collected anything related to English history.
Her friends teased her as an anglimaniac because she only watched British TV shows.
The novelist was almost an anglimaniac in her love of English culture.

senses.definition: The sport or hobby of fishing with a rod and line.
examples: He enjoys angling in mountain lakes during the summer.
Angling is a popular weekend activity in this region.
The magazine focuses on angling techniques and equipment.

senses.definition: Trying cleverly to get something you want, such as information, praise, or an advantage.
examples: She was angling for a promotion by taking on extra projects.
I think he’s angling for an invitation to the event.
The question sounded like he was angling for compliments.

senses.definition: Different ways of trying to get something you want in a clever or indirect way.
examples: His various anglings for attention were obvious to everyone.
The manager grew tired of their political anglings.
Her subtle anglings did not go unnoticed.

senses.definition: A form of English that tries to avoid words of foreign origin and use only native English words.
examples: The writer experimented with Anglish to see how it changed his style.
Some language enthusiasts promote Anglish as a purer form of English.
In Anglish, many common Latin-based words are replaced with Germanic ones.

senses.definition: A scholar or specialist in English language or literature.
examples: She works as an anglist at the university.
Many anglists focus on Shakespeare and other early authors.
As an anglist, he publishes articles on modern English novels.

senses.definition: The academic study of English language and literature.
examples: He is studying anglistics at the faculty of arts.
Anglistics covers linguistics, literature, and culture.
The department of anglistics offers several master’s programs.

senses.definition: Scholars or specialists in English language or literature.
examples: Many anglists attended the international conference on English studies.
The journal publishes work by anglists from around the world.
Anglists often collaborate with historians and cultural theorists.

senses.definition: Related to England or English people, especially in combination with another word.
examples: The city has a rich Anglo-French history.
They studied Anglo-American relations in the 20th century.
Anglo-Irish writers have had a big influence on modern literature.

senses.definition: A person of English or mainly English origin, especially in contrast to people from other language groups.
examples: In that region, Anglos live alongside Spanish speakers.
Some Anglos find it hard to learn the local language.
The festival attracts both Anglos and non-Anglos.

senses.definition: A rare term referring to English-speaking parts of the world treated as a single cultural area.
examples: The author used the term Anglogaea to describe the English-speaking world.
In his essay, Anglogaea stands for countries shaped by English culture.
Some critics argue that Anglogaea ignores local differences.

senses.definition: Related to Anglogaea or to the English-speaking world as a single cultural area.
examples: The book explores Anglogaean attitudes toward language.
She compared Anglogaean traditions with those of continental Europe.
Anglogaean culture has spread through media and trade.

senses.definition: Resembling or characteristic of English people or English things.
examples: The town had an angloid atmosphere despite being far from Britain.
Her accent sounded oddly angloid for someone raised abroad.
The film presents an angloid version of local history.

senses.definition: A man who greatly admires England or English things.
examples: He was an angloman who filled his house with British books and music.
The angloman in the story dreams of moving to London.
Critics called him an angloman for copying English customs.

senses.definition: A person who has an excessive or fashionable admiration for England or English culture.
examples: The young aristocrat was an anglomane who copied English fashions.
As an anglomane, she decorated her home in a very British style.
The novel mocks the anglomane who forgets his own traditions.

senses.definition: Very strong or excessive admiration for England or English things.
examples: In the 18th century, anglomania spread among the French upper classes.
The character’s anglomania leads him to copy every English habit.
Her anglomania is clear from her love of British television and music.

senses.definition: A person who shows extreme or obsessive admiration for England or English culture.
examples: The essayist called him an anglomaniac for his blind love of English customs.
Her friends joked that she was an anglomaniac who should have been born in London.
The film’s hero is an anglomaniac who dreams of attending Oxford.

senses.definition: People who show extreme or obsessive admiration for England or English culture.
examples: The club was full of anglomaniacs discussing British politics.
Writers joked that the city’s elites were all anglomaniacs.
In the story, the anglomaniacs try to change local customs.

senses.definition: Periods or cases of very strong admiration for England or English things.
examples: Different anglomanias have appeared in Europe over the centuries.
The historian describes several anglomanias in her book.
Cultural anglomanias often follow political changes.

senses.definition: A person who likes or admires England or English things.
examples: As an anglophil, he prefers British films and music.
Many anglophils visit London several times a year.
The club brings together anglophils from different countries.

senses.definition: A person who likes or strongly admires England, English people, or English culture.
examples: She has been an anglophile since childhood and loves British novels.
Many anglophiles enjoy watching British period dramas.
As an anglophile, he collects books about English history.

senses.definition: People who like or strongly admire England, English people, or English culture.
examples: The festival attracts anglophiles from all over the world.
Many anglophiles follow British news closely.
The tour is popular with anglophiles interested in royal history.

senses.definition: Strong admiration or love for England, English people, or English culture.
examples: Her anglophilia began when she started reading British novels.
The film reflects a kind of gentle anglophilia.
Anglophilia can influence how people see British politics.

senses.definition: A person who shows strong or extreme admiration for England or English culture.
examples: The author is an anglophiliac who sets all his stories in London.
Friends teased her as an anglophiliac for her love of British TV.
The club welcomes anglophiliacs from across the region.

senses.definition: Different cases or forms of strong admiration for England or English culture.
examples: The historian discusses various anglophilias in different countries.
These anglophilias shaped local views of British power.
Cultural anglophilias often appear in times of political change.

senses.definition: Showing or having a strong admiration for England or English culture.
examples: The novel has an anglophilic tone and setting.
Her anglophilic interests include British art and fashion.
The company’s anglophilic marketing targets British customers.

senses.definition: The quality or practice of admiring England or English culture.
examples: The critic sees anglophilism in the film’s romantic view of London.
Anglophilism was common in certain social circles.
The essay warns against uncritical anglophilism.

senses.definition: People who like or admire England or English things.
examples: The event was organised by local anglophils.
Anglophils often gather to watch British TV series.
Many anglophils learn British English at school.

senses.definition: Strong liking or admiration for England or English culture.
examples: His anglophily shows in his love of British comedy.
The article explores anglophily in modern popular culture.
Anglophily influenced the country’s foreign policy.

senses.definition: A person who strongly dislikes or is afraid of England or English things.
examples: He was accused of being an anglophobe after his remarks about British culture.
Anglophobes often criticise the influence of English worldwide.
The film portrays an anglophobe who rejects anything British.

senses.definition: People who strongly dislike or are afraid of England or English things.
examples: Some anglophobes criticise English influence in education.
The article discusses how anglophobes view globalisation.
Anglophobes disagree with closer ties to Britain.

senses.definition: Strong dislike or fear of England, English people, or English culture.
examples: The speech was criticised for encouraging anglophobia.
Anglophobia can affect international relations.
Some historians study the roots of anglophobia in Europe.

senses.definition: A person who shows strong or extreme dislike or fear of England or English things.
examples: The character is an anglophobiac who refuses to speak English.
An outspoken anglophobiac, he opposed all ties with Britain.
The journalist was labelled an anglophobiac after his article.

senses.definition: Different forms or cases of strong dislike or fear of England or English culture.
examples: The study compares anglophobias in various countries.
Historical anglophobias often grew during times of conflict.
These anglophobias influenced foreign policy decisions.

senses.definition: Showing strong dislike or fear of England, English people, or English culture.
examples: Some saw the article as deeply anglophobic.
Anglophobic attitudes can create tensions in international relations.
The film was criticised for its anglophobic message.

senses.definition: A person who strongly dislikes or opposes England or English influence.
examples: The politician was known as an anglophobist in his region.
Anglophobists criticised the trade agreement with the UK.
The writer is no anglophobist, but he worries about cultural dominance.

senses.definition: Using English as a main or official language.
examples: Canada has both anglophone and francophone regions.
Many anglophone countries cooperate on education projects.
He moved from a francophone area to an anglophone city.

senses.definition: A person whose main language is English, or who lives in an English-speaking community.
examples: Anglophones and francophones share this province.
The conference brings together anglophones from many countries.
As an anglophone, she helps newcomers practise English.

senses.definition: People whose main language is English, or who live in English-speaking communities.
examples: The city’s anglophones work closely with its other language groups.
Anglophones often help visitors with translation.
Many anglophones attend international schools.

senses.definition: Relating to English-speaking people or communities.
examples: The festival celebrates anglophonic and francophonic cultures.
Anglophonic media dominate the global market.
She researches anglophonic literature from Africa.

senses.definition: People of English or mainly English origin, especially in contrast to people from other language groups.
examples: In this region, Anglos and Hispanics live side by side.
Some Anglos struggle with learning the local language.
The survey shows differences between Anglos and non-Anglos.

senses.definition: A term with specialised or regional uses whose meaning depends on local context.
examples: The word ango is rarely found in standard English dictionaries.
In some texts, ango appears as a name or local term.
Without context, ango is difficult to interpret.

senses.definition: An old or rare spelling of ‘anguish’, meaning great mental suffering or pain.
examples: The poem speaks of deep angoise of the soul.
She wrote about her angoise in a series of letters.
His face showed angoise after the terrible news.

senses.definition: A country in southwest Africa, with Luanda as its capital.
examples: She travelled to Angola to work on a development project.
Angola is rich in natural resources such as oil and diamonds.
Portuguese is the official language of Angola.

senses.definition: Relating to Angola or its people.
examples: They enjoyed Angolan music at the festival.
Angolan culture blends many different traditions.
Angolan companies export oil to many countries.

senses.definition: A person from Angola.
examples: She is an Angolan working in the healthcare sector.
Angolans speak many local languages as well as Portuguese.
The team included Angolans and foreign experts.

senses.definition: People from Angola.
examples: Many Angolans live and work in other African countries.
Angolans celebrate their independence day with parades.
Young Angolans are hopeful about the future.

senses.definition: A member of a community on São Tomé Island, or the creole language spoken there, in specialised use.
examples: Researchers documented stories told by Angolar speakers.
The Angolar community has its own cultural traditions.
She wrote her thesis on the Angolar language.

senses.definition: An old or rare form meaning ‘Angolan’, relating to Angola or its people.
examples: The report used the term Angolese instead of Angolan.
She collected Angolese music recordings.
Angolese artists were featured in the exhibition.

senses.definition: A type of tree from eastern Australia, related to eucalyptus.
examples: An angophora grew at the edge of the rocky hill.
The garden includes several native angophora species.
They planted an angophora to provide shade.
